people.bio: Robert Evans Senior was a prominent Nottingham architect, partner in the firm Hine & Evans (with T.C. Hine). The firm designed Ravine House, 21 Lenton Road (c.1862), along with other notable buildings in The Park and across Nottingham. The lease of Ravine House was held by Robert Evans Senior and was assigned to his son Robert Evans Junior in July 1905.

Robert Evans Junior (b. c.1839; d. August 1927, aged 88) was a well-known Nottingham architect and son of Robert Evans Senior, himself a prominent architect and partner in the firm Hine & Evans. Robert Evans Junior practised in Nottingham for many years and was responsible for many notable buildings in the city.

He was the founder of the Nottingham Architectural Society, a past-president of the Nottinghamshire Architectural Association, and served for 35 years as honorary secretary of the Nottingham Society of Artists.

In July 1905, the lease of Ravine House, 21 Lenton Road — which had been built by his father's firm Hine & Evans c.1862 — was assigned from his father to Robert Evans Junior, who was already occupying the premises at that time. His death was reported in the Sheffield Daily Telegraph on Wednesday 17 August 1927.

bibliography.title: Obituary: Well-Known Nottingham Architect Dead
bibliography.notes: Wednesday 17 August 1927. Reports death of Robert Evans Junior at 24 Belvoir Drive, Sherwood, aged 88.
